method: Identical to baseline: faithful mirror tables with the exact hot-path index DDL, seeded at the same production-scale volumes, warmed, then EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, BUFFERS, TIMING ON) on the same 7 DB-layer query shapes. Same seed (/tmp/bench_seed.sql) and same probe script (/tmp/bench_explain.sql) as documented in the baseline reproduction.
honest_caveats: exec_ms values are DB-layer planner+execution times on a single warm connection, NOT end-to-end HTTP P50/P95/P99. Same measurement class as the baseline.

All shapes are sub-millisecond on warm mirror tables. Run-to-run variance at this scale (tenths of a microsecond to ~0.15ms) EXCEEDS any real signal, so per-scenario exec_ms deltas below are NOT claimed as genuine speedups. The load-bearing result is the PLAN (index hit / seq-scan absence), not the microsecond delta.

Live latency/QPS/memory/cache-hit-rate remain NOT captured this run — no built+running homeserver on :8008 with seeded scenario data was available, exactly as in the baseline. These dimensions are unchanged in coverage, not measured-and-flat.
Mirror tables carry the production index definitions but no FK/trigger/write-amplification overhead.

The `user_id = ANY(ARRAY(SELECT ... generate_series))` shape (Scenario 6) drives planner choice between plain Index Scan and Bitmap Heap Scan; the choice flips run-to-run and is not controlled by any code change on this branch.

explain_verification.note: Same dataset and shapes as baseline. exec_ms = EXPLAIN ANALYZE Execution Time. ALL shapes hit the intended index; ZERO seq scans — index strategy preserved end-to-end after 24 optimization tasks.

queries.annotation: flat (sub-ms noise; plan variant Bitmap vs baseline plain Index Scan, both index-served)

flat (sub-ms noise). Planner still prefers the narrow idx_events_room_stream_ordering over idx_events_sync_covering at LIMIT 100 — matches baseline observation; covering index does not win at this LIMIT.

WATCH (sub-ms, still index-served). This run's planner chose a Bitmap Heap Scan (301 buffers) vs the baseline's plain Index Scan (204 buffers); the ANY(ARRAY(subquery)) shape flips plans run-to-run. Not a code regression — no device_keys read path changed on this branch. Absolute cost remains 0.6ms.

queries.plan: Index Scan idx_one_time_keys_user_device (Filter: NOT used AND algorithm)

n_plus_1_verification.method: Structural query-count proof by reading the batch helpers (not runtime pg_stat_statements — no live traffic this run).
n_plus_1_verification.before: delete_devices_batch looped per device, calling delete_lazy_loaded_members_for_device + record_device_list_change once EACH → 2*N queries for N deleted devices.
n_plus_1_verification.after: Rows are grouped by user_id (HashMap), then per user-group exactly ONE call each to: delete_lazy_loaded_members_for_devices_batch (single `DELETE ... WHERE user_id=$1 AND device_id = ANY($2)`, mod.rs:347) and record_device_list_changes_batch (single CTE `INSERT ... UNNEST($3::TEXT[])`, mod.rs:202).
n_plus_1_verification.query_count: 2 queries per DISTINCT user_id, independent of device count (was 2*N).
n_plus_1_verification.annotation: PASS. For the common single-user multi-device delete, query count drops from 2*N to 2. Covered by test test_delete_devices_batch_uses_batch_side_effects (creates 3 devices, deletes batch, asserts 3 device_lists_changes rows written via the batch path).

measured_dimensions.db_query_plan_latency: captured (EXPLAIN ANALYZE) — no regression, 7/7 index hits
measured_dimensions.db_query_count_per_endpoint: structural (device delete path verified; others per audit-04 inventory, not runtime-counted)
measured_dimensions.http_p50_p95_p99: NOT captured (no running server) — same as baseline
measured_dimensions.cache_hit_rate: NOT captured (no live traffic) — same as baseline

verdict.db_layer: No hot-path shape regressed at the plan level. All 7 still index-served, 0 seq scans. Absolute exec_ms deltas are within sub-ms run-to-run noise and are reported as FLAT, not as speedups.
watch_items: Scenario 6 (device_keys ANY) chose a bitmap plan this run at ~0.6ms — planner variance on the ANY(ARRAY(subquery)) shape, not a code change; re-check under a real running server with pg_stat_statements.
verdict.n_plus_1: OPT-012 device delete N+1 structurally eliminated (2*N -> 2 queries per user).
verdict.live_latency: p50/p95/p99, QPS, cache-hit-rate, memory still require a running homeserver + seed script (none exists) — carried forward from baseline next_steps, NOT achievable in this environment.
